prose:
Method provenance block
Property: Most electrophilic Fukui index (f⁺) for benzaldehyde, per-atom value shown above and maximal at atom 3 (C2 in the carbonyl carbon counting O as atom 1).
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RI density fitting switched on by chemkit default).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied benzaldehyde.xyz (14 atoms); no geometry optimisation — all Fukui single-points run at this fixed geometry (DFT//input geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet for the N-electron reference (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation and anion states at charge +1/–1, multiplicity 2 each.
Software: chemkit 2026.7 backend, PySCF 2.x + ASE wrappers.
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for the N, N+1, and N–1 single points all converged (chemkit set default tight SCF).  No engine-level warnings list was returned, but three integrity checks were flagged as warnings and are reproduced verbatim below.

Live log: the calculation wrote live output to
/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_o3/20260702-201300_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/fukui_20260702-202204.out (still on disk, can be inspected with tail ‑f or opened in full).

Headline result
Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C) with f⁺ = 0.39938967 (dimensionless).
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O) with f⁻ = 0.23839906 (dimensionless).
